admin.title: Review queue

developer.title: Publish your games on GameHub
developer.subtitle: Become a developer to upload HTML5 games, ship updates, and get a live leaderboard for every title.
developer.perk1Body: A private dashboard to create games, upload builds, and track review status.
developer.perk2Body: Every published game gets score tracking with daily, weekly, and all-time boards.
developer.perk3Body: Ship a new version anytime — your live game keeps running until the update is approved.
developer.step1Body: In Studio, add your game's name, category, screen orientation, and how scores rank (high or low wins). This creates a DRAFT only you can see.
developer.step2Body: Zip your game so that index.html sits at the root of the archive. Everything it needs — scripts, art, sounds — must be inside the zip; games run sandboxed with no outside network access.
developer.step3Body: Upload the .zip against a version number like 1.0.0. Each version needs a unique number, and you can upload as many as you like.
developer.step4Body: Once your draft has at least one uploaded version, send it to the review queue. Metadata is locked while a game is in review.
developer.step5Body: An admin test-plays your build and either publishes it or sends it back with a reason so you can fix and resubmit. Approved games are playable by everyone.
developer.step6Body: Uploading a new version of a published game queues it for review automatically. Players keep playing the current version until the update is approved.
developer.req1: Your game is built with HTML5 / JavaScript and runs in the browser.
developer.req2: index.html is at the root of the zip and loads without any external server.
developer.req3: You own or have the rights to publish the game and all of its assets.
developer.applyHint: Tell us what you'd like to publish — a short pitch or a link to your work. Optional, but it helps us review faster.
developer.messagePlaceholder: I make arcade games — here's a link to my portfolio…
developer.submitted: Request sent — we'll review it soon.
developer.loginRequired: Log in to apply for the Developer role.
developer.pendingBody: Thanks for applying! An admin will review your request soon — Studio access will appear here once it's approved.
developer.rejectedRetry: You can update your pitch below and apply again.
